hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The public prompt only describes replay-sensitive audit drift. The solver must inspect Rust modules and run scenarios to discover how late rows, restart state, retained emit markers, and ordering interact.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: A working repair spans input folding, state reconstruction, delayed emission, and JSON audit output. No single module owns the complete behavior.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose
hardness_axes.reasoning: The instruction will state mismatched audit outcomes, not the underlying checkpoint, ordering, or identity defects. The solver has to infer the failure causes from code and runtime output.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: A local change that merely suppresses duplicates can erase legitimate corrections; a local ordering change can change lineage identities. The repair has to preserve coupled invariants across paths.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: This is not a textbook window exercise. The hard part is reconstructing the engine's bespoke replay contract across partition-local state, delayed correction rows, and compaction side effects.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Even an honest prompt can say direct and restarted audits must agree without naming the internal authorities or formulas. The solver still has to reconstruct which code paths disagree.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The verifier will cover several named cases, all generated through the same engine. Difficulty is not finding one corrupted row.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Passing requires source changes across multiple Rust modules rather than replacing one output, one manifest, or one fixture.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The same implementation must handle duplicate replay, late updates, partition independence, retained state, and compaction-order cases.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The prompt can honestly describe the JSON audit and agreement criteria while withholding internal state reconstruction details that would collapse the diagnosis.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Anti-cheating guards are not the hardness source; the substantive work is making the engine deterministic under replay instead of hardcoding the audit.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The idea is not about verifier wiring, timeouts, Docker image setup, or reward files. It is about a nontrivial stateful data-processing bug.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned implementation distributes the repair across at least four Rust roots. A one-function patch will leave test subsets failing.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle will modify several source files with new logic and avoid simply replacing a short helper or shipping a precomputed report.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The repair is not a table, threshold map, or policy knob. It changes the way rows are reconstructed, folded, emitted, and serialized.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path symbols and directories are preassigned opaque names, and the instruction nouns are banned from those code symbols.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The future code will not expose helpers named after the public contract. It uses neutral symbols such as fold_a, merge_b, mark_c, load_d, and emit_e.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is not a standard safe extractor, schema validator, or stock streaming recipe. It tests a bespoke recovery invariant in an existing codebase.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No security aura is being used. The difficulty claim rests on event-time replay semantics and state coupling.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The checks are coupled: deduplication, ordering, retained emit markers, and correction identity cannot be repaired independently without affecting each other.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Docker, shell scripts, and generated cases are support structure only. The real work is in the Rust engine.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Opening the obvious command script will not reveal the solution. The solver must trace runtime output through several modules and state surfaces.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The concrete plan requires professional debugging of stateful replay behavior and remains above medium after honest disclosure.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The evidence lists more than three concrete code/runtime discoveries that are not stated in instruction.md.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned instruction is symptoms-only: it names the audit drift and output surface but not the implementation cause or fix path.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three valid fix topologies are enumerated, and each requires at least three coordinating locations with no single sufficient file.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: The task is checked by deterministic pytest tests over a Rust binary's JSON output and repeated scenario runs.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The public contract can clearly state the audit command, output path, schema, and agreement properties without naming internal causes.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can solve it by tracing the engine and implementing consistent state handling in a few hours.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: The task forces diagnosis across restart and late-correction semantics, not transcription of a complete formula.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Streaming aggregation and replay-correct late-event output are practical reliability concerns for data systems.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The verifier judges the resulting behavior and accepts any implementation that produces correct replay-stable audit output.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md tells the solver what clean output looks like, but not how the engine partitions input, restores state, determines correction identity, or orders retained rows.

discovery_budget.discovery: The restarted path reconstructs part of its state from persisted rows while the uninterrupted path keeps in-memory markers, and the two authorities are not equivalent.
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/src/d3/host.rs and runtime state files under /app/state
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the state-authority split would point directly at the recovery fix.

discovery_budget.discovery: Rows that arrive after a case has already emitted a value need stable correction identity tied to the same ordered input ids, not to incidental processing order.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Stating this would turn the task into implementing a named identity recipe.

discovery_budget.discovery: The compaction pass reorders retained rows differently from the direct pass, so lineage order has to be derived from event-time metadata rather than file order.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Calling out compaction order would make the edit frontier too obvious.

discovery_budget.discovery: Partition-local progress must not suppress a late update from a different partition that shares the same logical key and window.
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/src/a0/core.rs and mixed partition scenario inputs
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: This is one of the core diagnosis steps and should be recovered by reading the code and comparing cases.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The instruction will describe mismatched audit output, output schema, and cleanliness criteria without naming algorithms, thresholds, internal modules, or the repair.

attack_path.summary: A strong solver might try to make direct and replay reports equal by sorting every output or dropping repeated corrections.
attack_path.reasoning: That attack fails because tests independently derive totals, require retained legitimate correction rows, and check duplicate suppression without losing partition-local updates.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: The smallest successful patch rewires folding, retained-state loading, delayed-row ordering, and emission identity across several Rust files.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: A one-line sort, a global dedupe set, or a report-level equality hack cannot satisfy late correction values, lineage order, partition isolation, and repeated-run idempotence together.

collapse_audit.reasoning: The future editable frontier is distributed and opaque, the public prompt is symptoms-only, and expected values are derived by tests from scenario semantics rather than shipped as visible goldens.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: After the file tree is visible, the solver still has to infer which state surface is authoritative across direct and replay paths, preserve legitimate late corrections while suppressing duplicates, and make correction identity stable under compaction order changes.

renames_during_drafting.reason: The original symbol contained an instruction noun.
The original name revealed a planned internal authority.
The original test name contained instruction nouns.
